content: read reviews/1040.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/1040.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/1040.md.

# Review: deepmind/1040.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19‚Äď38).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 1040`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1040`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (infimum of Lebesgue measure of sublevel sets of monic polynomials with roots in a closed set, and its relationship to transfinite diameter).
- [x] Optional bibliography entry present: `[EHP58]` ‚ÄĒ ErdŇĎs, Herzog, and Piranian, *Metric properties of polynomials*, J. Analyse Math. **6** (1958), 125‚Äď148.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1040 ... end Erdos1040` (lines 44, 83).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1040`.
- [x] `open scoped ENNReal` and `open MeasureTheory Classical Filter Finset` appear **before** the namespace block (lines 40‚Äď42).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `pairwiseDistProd` (line 48) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 46‚Äď47).
- [x] `nthTransfiniteDiam` (line 54) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 52‚Äď53).
- [x] `transfiniteDiameter` (line 60) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 58‚Äď59).
- [x] `sublevelMeasure` (line 66) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 63‚Äď65).
- [x] All four definitions are marked `noncomputable`.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] All functions use `lowerCamelCase`: `pairwiseDistProd`, `nthTransfiniteDiam`, `transfiniteDiameter`, `sublevelMeasure`.
- N/A: No custom types or Props defined (no `UpperCamelCase` check needed).
- N/A: No `local notation` used.
- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks added (optional).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword: `theorem erdos_1040`.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_1040` (matches namespace number).
- [x] Name follows `snake_case` convention for terms of Props.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 28 30]` (line 77).
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 70‚Äď76) describing the conjecture and referencing `[EHP58, p.135]`.
- [x] Theorem docstring references a source paper: `[EHP58, p.135]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks "Is $\mu(F) = 0$ whenever the transfinite diameter of $F$ is $\geq 1$?" ‚ÄĒ this is a yes/no question, so **Case 1** applies.

- [x] The theorem type is `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ P`, where `P` formalizes the question as asked. Correct.
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: `‚ąÄ (F : Set ‚Ąā), IsClosed F ‚Üí F.Infinite ‚Üí transfiniteDiameter F ‚Č• 1 ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (őľ : Measure ‚Ąā) [őľ.IsAddHaarMeasure], sublevelMeasure F őľ = 0`. With `answer(True)` this would mean "yes, $\mu(F) = 0$ whenever the conditions hold."
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters. All quantifiers (`‚ąÄ F`, `‚ąÄ őľ`) are on the RHS of `‚ÜĒ`.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]`: `category research open`.
- [x] Value is valid: `research open` is in the allowed list.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body (line 81).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 28 30`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed (two: 28, 30).
- [x] Numbers are in ascending numeric order: 28 < 30.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both 28 (Measure and integration) and 30 (Functions of a complex variable) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjecture uses `by sorry` form (`:= by\n  sorry` on lines 80‚Äď81, multi-line form is acceptable).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- N/A: File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. The problem is a single yes/no question, so no variants are expected.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚Ąā`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚Ąē`, `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚Č•`, `‚ÜĒ`, `‚Üí`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (longest line ~80 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] Not verified ‚ÄĒ requires `lake build` execution.
- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified.

---

## Summary

**Status: PASS (modulo build verification)**

The file is excellently structured and conforms to all checklist requirements. The formalization captures ErdŇĎs Problem 1040 faithfully: for every closed infinite set $F \subseteq \mathbb{C}$ with transfinite diameter $\geq 1$, the infimum of Lebesgue measure of $\{z : |f(z)| < 1\}$ over monic polynomials with roots in $F$ is zero. The mathematical setup is clean ‚ÄĒ `pairwiseDistProd`, `nthTransfiniteDiam`, and `transfiniteDiameter` build up the transfinite diameter step by step, while `sublevelMeasure` captures the infimum over all monic polynomials (using `Fin (n+1)` to ensure at least one root). All helper definitions are properly documented, correctly cased, and marked `noncomputable`. The `answer()` usage is correct (Case 1 ‚ÄĒ yes/no question with `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ P`). AMS codes 28 (Measure and integration) and 30 (Functions of a complex variable) are well-chosen for this problem involving Lebesgue measure and polynomial sublevel sets in $\mathbb{C}$. The bibliography entry `[EHP58]` is included in both the module docstring and the theorem docstring.

No issues found.

    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 1040
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1040](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1040)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $F \subseteq \mathbb{C}$ be a closed infinite set, and let $\mu(F)$ be the infimum of
    25‚Üí$|\{z : |f(z)| < 1\}|$ (Lebesgue measure), as $f$ ranges over all polynomials of
    26‚Üíthe shape $\prod(z - z_i)$ with $z_i \in F$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíIs $\mu(F) = 0$ whenever the transfinite diameter of $F$ is $\geq 1$?
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíThe transfinite diameter (logarithmic capacity) of $F$ is defined by
    31‚Üí$$\rho(F) = \lim_{n \to \infty} \sup_{z_1,\ldots,z_n \in F} \left(\prod_{i<j} |z_i - z_j|\right)^{1/\binom{n}{2}}.$$
    32‚Üí
    33‚ÜíErdŇĎs, Herzog, and Piranian showed the answer is yes when $F$ is a line segment
    34‚Üíor disc.
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí[EHP58] ErdŇĎs, P., Herzog, F., and Piranian, G., *Metric properties of polynomials*,
    37‚ÜíJ. Analyse Math. **6** (1958), 125‚Äď148.
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üíopen scoped ENNReal
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üíopen MeasureTheory Classical Filter Finset
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üínamespace Erdos1040
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/-- The product of pairwise distances $\prod_{i<j} \|z_i - z_j\|$ for a tuple of
    47‚Üícomplex numbers. -/
    48‚Üínoncomputable def pairwiseDistProd {n : ‚Ąē} (z : Fin n ‚Üí ‚Ąā) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    49‚Üí  ((univ : Finset (Fin n √ó Fin n)).filter (fun p => p.1 < p.2)).prod
    50‚Üí    (fun p => ‚ÄĖz p.1 - z p.2‚ÄĖ)
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí/-- The $n$-th transfinite diameter of $F \subseteq \mathbb{C}$:
    53‚Üí$d_n(F) = \sup_{z_1,\ldots,z_n \in F} \left(\prod_{i<j} |z_i - z_j|\right)^{2/(n(n-1))}$. -/
    54‚Üínoncomputable def nthTransfiniteDiam (F : Set ‚Ąā) (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    55‚Üí  sSup {t : ‚ĄĚ | ‚ąÉ z : Fin n ‚Üí ‚Ąā, (‚ąÄ i, z i ‚ąą F) ‚ąß
    56‚Üí    t = (pairwiseDistProd z) ^ ((2 : ‚ĄĚ) / (‚ÜĎn * (‚ÜĎn - 1)))}
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí/-- The transfinite diameter (logarithmic capacity) of $F \subseteq \mathbb{C}$:
    59‚Üí$\rho(F) = \lim_{n \to \infty} d_n(F)$. -/
    60‚Üínoncomputable def transfiniteDiameter (F : Set ‚Ąā) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    61‚Üí  lim (atTop.map (fun n => nthTransfiniteDiam F n))
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí/-- The sublevel set measure $\mu(F, \mu)$: infimum of $\mu(\{z : \|f(z)\| < 1\})$ over
    64‚Üíall monic polynomials with roots in $F$. Uses `Fin (n+1)` to ensure at least
    65‚Üíone root. -/
    66‚Üínoncomputable def sublevelMeasure (F : Set ‚Ąā) (őľ : Measure ‚Ąā) : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě :=
    67‚Üí  ‚®Ö (n : ‚Ąē) (z : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí ‚Ąā) (_ : ‚ąÄ i, z i ‚ąą F),
    68‚Üí    őľ {w : ‚Ąā | ‚ÄĖ(univ : Finset (Fin (n + 1))).prod (fun i => w - z i)‚ÄĖ < 1}
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí/--
    71‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 1040 [EHP58, p.135]:
    72‚Üí
    73‚ÜíIs it true that for every closed infinite set $F \subseteq \mathbb{C}$ with transfinite
    74‚Üídiameter $\geq 1$, the infimum of the Lebesgue measure of $\{z : |f(z)| < 1\}$
    75‚Üíover monic polynomials with all roots in $F$ is zero?
    76‚Üí-/
    77‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 28 30]
    78‚Üítheorem erdos_1040 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    79‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (F : Set ‚Ąā), IsClosed F ‚Üí F.Infinite ‚Üí transfiniteDiameter F ‚Č• 1 ‚Üí
    80‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (őľ : Measure ‚Ąā) [őľ.IsAddHaarMeasure], sublevelMeasure F őľ = 0 := by
    81‚Üí  sorry
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üíend Erdos1040
    84‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 1040

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1040](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1040)

Let $F \subseteq \mathbb{C}$ be a closed infinite set, and let $\mu(F)$ be the infimum of
$|\{z : |f(z)| < 1\}|$ (Lebesgue measure), as $f$ ranges over all polynomials of
the shape $\prod(z - z_i)$ with $z_i \in F$.

Is $\mu(F) = 0$ whenever the transfinite diameter of $F$ is $\geq 1$?

The transfinite diameter (logarithmic capacity) of $F$ is defined by
$$\rho(F) = \lim_{n \to \infty} \sup_{z_1,\ldots,z_n \in F} \left(\prod_{i<j} |z_i - z_j|\right)^{1/\binom{n}{2}}.$$

ErdŇĎs, Herzog, and Piranian showed the answer is yes when $F$ is a line segment
or disc.

[EHP58] ErdŇĎs, P., Herzog, F., and Piranian, G., *Metric properties of polynomials*,
J. Analyse Math. **6** (1958), 125‚Äď148.
-/

open scoped ENNReal

open MeasureTheory Classical Filter Finset

namespace Erdos1040

/-- The product of pairwise distances $\prod_{i<j} \|z_i - z_j\|$ for a tuple of
complex numbers. -/
noncomputable def pairwiseDistProd {n : ‚Ąē} (z : Fin n ‚Üí ‚Ąā) : ‚ĄĚ :=
  ((univ : Finset (Fin n √ó Fin n)).filter (fun p => p.1 < p.2)).prod
    (fun p => ‚ÄĖz p.1 - z p.2‚ÄĖ)

/-- The $n$-th transfinite diameter of $F \subseteq \mathbb{C}$:
$d_n(F) = \sup_{z_1,\ldots,z_n \in F} \left(\prod_{i<j} |z_i - z_j|\right)^{2/(n(n-1))}$. -/
noncomputable def nthTransfiniteDiam (F : Set ‚Ąā) (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
  sSup {t : ‚ĄĚ | ‚ąÉ z : Fin n ‚Üí ‚Ąā, (‚ąÄ i, z i ‚ąą F) ‚ąß
    t = (pairwiseDistProd z) ^ ((2 : ‚ĄĚ) / (‚ÜĎn * (‚ÜĎn - 1)))}

/-- The transfinite diameter (logarithmic capacity) of $F \subseteq \mathbb{C}$:
$\rho(F) = \lim_{n \to \infty} d_n(F)$. -/
noncomputable def transfiniteDiameter (F : Set ‚Ąā) : ‚ĄĚ :=
  lim (atTop.map (fun n => nthTransfiniteDiam F n))

/-- The sublevel set measure $\mu(F, \mu)$: infimum of $\mu(\{z : \|f(z)\| < 1\})$ over
all monic polynomials with roots in $F$. Uses `Fin (n+1)` to ensure at least
one root. -/
noncomputable def sublevelMeasure (F : Set ‚Ąā) (őľ : Measure ‚Ąā) : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě :=
  ‚®Ö (n : ‚Ąē) (z : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí ‚Ąā) (_ : ‚ąÄ i, z i ‚ąą F),
    őľ {w : ‚Ąā | ‚ÄĖ(univ : Finset (Fin (n + 1))).prod (fun i => w - z i)‚ÄĖ < 1}

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 1040 [EHP58, p.135]:

Is it true that for every closed infinite set $F \subseteq \mathbb{C}$ with transfinite
diameter $\geq 1$, the infimum of the Lebesgue measure of $\{z : |f(z)| < 1\}$
over monic polynomials with all roots in $F$ is zero?
-/
@[category research open, AMS 28 30]
theorem erdos_1040 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    ‚ąÄ (F : Set ‚Ąā), IsClosed F ‚Üí F.Infinite ‚Üí transfiniteDiameter F ‚Č• 1 ‚Üí
    ‚ąÄ (őľ : Measure ‚Ąā) [őľ.IsAddHaarMeasure], sublevelMeasure F őľ = 0 := by
  sorry

end Erdos1040
